title: Wild White World
subtitle: A First Look at Polar Animals
author: Hannah Whitfield
subject: polar animals and the icy white places they live
flux_style: Soft hand-painted watercolour storybook illustration in an adorable, sweet kawaii style — large friendly sparkly eyes, a gentle happy face, soft edges and tender huggable charm — with every animal drawn in its OWN correct natural body shape and true proportions for its real species (flat animals stay flat, long animals stay long, tall animals stay tall, never forced into a uniform round chubby ball), loose simplified naive childlike style, a soft cool polar palette of gentle snow-whites, icy blues, soft periwinkle and lavender shadows on the snow, pale aqua glacier ice and touches of warm rose-gold and peach polar-twilight light, soft tender watercolour washes, gentle visible brushwork, soft edges, painterly flat shapes, bright friendly, the main animal subject reads clearly and stands out from its background through gentle tonal and value separation and a soft natural grounding shadow, with clean clearly-defined edges and form, while the snowy background behind it is painted with soft blue and lavender shadow so it gently recedes and a pale or white animal never disappears into the white snow; each animal's coat stays smooth, simple, plain and true to its real species with no invented spots, speckles or extra markings unless that species truly has them, so the subject stands out from the setting without ever blending or washing into it, children's picture book art, not photorealistic, not a photograph, no text
negative_prompt: text, words, letters, numbers, captions, watermark, signature, artist signature, logo, initials, frame, border, white paper border, blank white margins, unpainted paper edges, vignette, faded edges, washed-out pale sky, blown-out overexposed highlights, bleached faded colours, whiteout, empty sparse composition, large blank empty background area, tiny subject, subject stuck in a corner, deformed, malformed anatomy, extra limbs, blurry, low quality, photorealistic, photograph, 3d render, glossy plastic, harsh outlines, heavy black outlines, stray background creatures, extra unrelated animals, random extra birds in the background, sprig, branch, twig, leaves, berries, flower or any growth sprouting from the head or back, antennae, oversaturated colours, neon glow, garish vivid high-saturation palette, glitter, sparkle glyphs, four-pointed star sparkles, star-field of tiny white speckles, glitter dust on the animal's body, ringed tail, banded tail, striped tail, black-and-white ring-tailed lemur tail, raccoon ringed tail, bushy ringed tail
topics: a fluffy white polar bear cub with a soft rounded body, small round ears and big gentle dark eyes, sitting happily on the bright snow of the frozen arctic
a plump emperor penguin with a smooth black back, a white front and a warm golden-orange patch on each side of its neck, standing tall on the blue-white sea ice
a small fluffy arctic fox with a thick snow-white coat, a pointed little face and a big soft bushy tail, curled up cosily on the soft blue-shadowed snow
a fluffy white harp seal pup with a round body, big dark shining eyes and little dark whiskers, resting softly on the bright sea ice
a round snowy owl with soft white feathers flecked with tiny dark markings, a small hooked beak and big round yellow eyes, perched calmly on a snowy rock
a gentle reindeer with a soft warm-brown coat, a pale chest and a tall branching pair of antlers, standing quietly in the snowy tundra
a fluffy white arctic hare with a soft rounded body, short ears and big dark eyes, sitting still on the frosty pale-blue snow
a shaggy musk ox with a thick dark-brown woolly coat hanging low, a broad woolly face and two smooth curved horns, standing calmly on the windswept tundra
a little Atlantic puffin with a plump black-and-white body, bright orange feet and a large colourful orange-and-blue triangular beak, standing on a rocky cliff by the cold blue sea
a smooth round white beluga whale with a gentle rounded forehead and a soft friendly smile, floating at the surface of the calm deep-blue arctic sea among pale drifting ice
a plump little ptarmigan with soft fluffy white winter feathers, feathered feet and a tiny black beak, resting on the pale blue-shadowed snow
a sturdy white Dall sheep with a thick woolly coat and a pair of thick curved amber horns, standing on a snowy rocky slope
a young moose with a long warm-brown body, long legs, big rounded ears and a broad soft muzzle, standing gently at the snowy edge of a frozen spruce forest
a walrus calf with a smooth round body, two short pale tusks and soft wide whiskers, resting calmly on the blue-white sea ice
a fluffy grey-and-white emperor penguin chick with a round downy body and a soft dark-and-white face, standing on the pale sea ice
a slender little ermine with a soft snow-white winter coat, bright dark eyes and one small black tip at the very end of its tail, sitting upright on the bright snow
a graceful white tundra swan with a long smooth gently curved neck, soft white feathers and a black beak, resting on the calm icy-blue water
a plump ringed seal with a silvery-grey coat marked with soft pale rings, big dark eyes and short whiskers, resting on the blue-white sea ice
a little arctic ground squirrel with soft sandy-brown fur, small rounded ears and bright dark eyes, sitting up on the snow-dusted tundra ground
a tiny round lemming with soft golden-brown and grey fur, small ears and bright dark eyes, sitting on the mossy snow-dusted tundra
art_prompt: a soft watercolour storybook front cover: ONE single adorable, joyful fluffy white polar bear cub as the BIG central hero of the cover, large and prominent and filling most of the frame, centred in the composition and taking up the majority of the picture, smiling a big warm happy smile with large sparkly friendly dark eyes and soft rosy cheeks, a soft fluffy snow-white coat and small round ears, sitting cheerfully on the bright snow of a sunny arctic landscape; soft snowdrifts with gentle blue and lavender shadows, distant pale-blue glacier ice and a soft rose-gold polar-twilight sky, the background richly and evenly painted from top to bottom all the way to every edge with soft snow-whites, icy blues and warm twilight light (no other animals, no people); the cub's head, ears, back and body are completely smooth, clean and bare with nothing sprouting from them; the cub looks happy, warm, gentle, sweet and inviting (a cheerful friendly smile, definitely NOT sad, NOT scary, NOT angry, NOT sleepy); exaggerated kawaii cuteness, the whole frame richly painted in gentle snow-whites, icy blues and warm rose-gold twilight, bright cheerful and inviting, no washed-out or blank empty areas, no text, no words
blurb: Step into a wild white world! From a fluffy polar bear cub on the sparkling snow to a little puffin on a rocky shore, this gentle first arctic book takes early readers out across the ice and tundra to meet twenty amazing animals in the frosty white places they call home. Soft, glowing watercolours and simple, read-aloud rhymes make every page a warm discovery — a perfect first polar adventure for curious little ones.
